input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the setup:

**AppShell.xaml**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyApp.Pages"
    Shell.FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page, no tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**
```csharp
namespace MyApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register route for detail page (not in the flyout/visual hierarchy)
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Pages.ProductDetailsPage),
                              typeof(Pages.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating to the details page** (e.g., from `ActiveProductsPage`):

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(ProductDetailsPage),
    new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Product"] = selectedProduct });
```

Receive the parameter with `[QueryProperty]`:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(Product), "Product")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public Product Product { get; set; }
}
```

**Key points:**

- `FlyoutItem` → top-level flyout entry. Nesting `Tab` elements inside gives you the bottom tab bar under that entry.
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until navigated to.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` is for pages **outside** the visual hierarchy—Shell resolves hierarchical pages automatically, but detail pages pushed onto the stack need explicit registration.
